author: orchestrator
builder_claim: DuckDB analytical query tool (duckdb_query) — a safe, gated, read-only raw SQL passthrough tool for agent-facing DuckDB queries, registered in ToolRegistry behind experimentalDuckDBQuery flag, with consolidated SQL firewall, subprocess timeout, and stress repairs applied

required_proof.description: Demonstrate that the duckdb_query tool is safely wired into ToolRegistry behind experimental flag, with SQL firewall consolidation, LIMIT enforcement, missing-binary handling, and subprocess timeout

evidence.description: Claim atom verification and test results

alternatives_rejected.rejection_evidence: analytics implies higher-level functionality than raw SQL passthrough. duckdb_query is honest about the backend and operation type.
DuckDB binary may not be installed. Feature flag prevents crashes and follows codebase conventions (experimentalCoordination, experimentalScout, etc.).

alternatives_rejected.alternative: Route through DatabaseAdapter.Service instead of DuckDB.Service
alternatives_rejected.rejection_evidence: DatabaseAdapter.Service tag conflict prevents DuckDBAdapter from being merged into AppLayer. DuckDB.Service (@opencode/DuckDB) is a separate tag with no conflict.

intended_released_boundary.consumer_purpose: Enable read-only analytical SQL queries against DuckDB for agent-assisted debugging and introspection
atoms: duckdb_query tool registered in ToolRegistry behind experimentalDuckDBQuery flag
Consolidated SQL firewall module (duckdb-firewall.ts) shared by db.duckdb.ts and adapter.ts
Missing DuckDB binary → human-readable install guide error
Pool with queue timeout and execution timeout

implemented_capabilities: duckdb_query tool definition with Schema.Struct parameter validation
LIMIT enforcement via regex detection + append
Missing-binary detection with brew/apt/winget install guide
Consolidated SQL firewall (10 blocked functions + COPY…TO)
Firewall false-positive reduction (single-line comment + string literal stripping)
14 firewall unit tests, 4 tool tests, 3 pool-timeout tests = 21 total
capabilities_explicitly_not_implemented: Integration tests with real duckdb binary (CI-gated)
external_effects_not_executed: git push — checkpoint not created from restricted tool environment
strongest_suspected_weakness: Pool timeout (F-001/F-004) is tested only with isolated error construction tests, not with actual duckdb subprocess timing. Integration tests are CI-gated and skipped when duckdb binary is absent.
proposed_reviewer_attack_surface: Subprocess timeout — does proc.kill() actually work on macOS/Linux/Windows?
AbortSignal cleanup — is the event listener properly cleaned up on all exit paths?
Firewall false-positive reduction — do the comment/string regexes cover all edge cases?
Effect.orDie outer wrapper — is the InvalidArgumentsError actually surfaced to the LLM?
Feature flag wiring — does the tool actually appear/disappear based on env var?

round_1.result: 2 blocking defects (B-001 missing pool-timeout tests, B-002 missing spawn-error test)
round_1.repairs_applied: Created duckdb-pool-timeout.test.ts (3 tests). Added spawn-error → InvalidArgumentsError test to duckdb-query.test.ts

round_2.repairs_applied: All 2 blocking defects repaired and verified. 7/7 new tests pass.
